title: Auto Repair Quote Consistency Calculator
description: Industrial-grade tool to assess consistency of auto repair quotes against industry benchmarks (ISO, Lean, Six Sigma principles).

inputs.label: Labor Rate per Hour
inputs.businessContext: Standard hourly labor charge from the repair shop.

businessContext_i18n.en: Standard hourly labor charge from the repair shop.

inputs.label: Estimated Labor Hours
inputs.businessContext: Total labor hours quoted for the repair job.

businessContext_i18n.en: Total labor hours quoted for the repair job.

inputs.label: Parts Cost
inputs.businessContext: Total cost of replacement parts as quoted.

businessContext_i18n.en: Total cost of replacement parts as quoted.

inputs.label: Sales Tax Rate
inputs.businessContext: Applicable sales tax percentage on parts and supplies.

businessContext_i18n.en: Applicable sales tax percentage on parts and supplies.

inputs.label: Shop Type
inputs.businessContext: Type of repair shop influences benchmark labor rates.

businessContext_i18n.en: Type of repair shop influences benchmark labor rates.

inputs.label: Repair Category
inputs.businessContext: Category of repair affects typical labor hours and parts markup.

businessContext_i18n.en: Category of repair affects typical labor hours and parts markup.

inputs.label: Use Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM) Parts
inputs.businessContext: OEM parts are typically more expensive than aftermarket.

businessContext_i18n.en: OEM parts are typically more expensive than aftermarket.

rules.message: Labor rate must be between 50 and 250 USD/hour.
Labor hours must be between 0.5 and 40.
Parts cost must be between 0 and 10,000 USD.
Shop supplies fee must be between 0 and 500 USD.
Tax rate must be between 0% and 15%.
OEM parts cost seems too low; verify parts cost.

thresholds.message: Total quote exceeds $5,000. Consider obtaining a second opinion.
Labor hours exceed 10. Verify scope of work.
Parts cost exceeds $3,000. Check for alternative parts.
Quote consistency score is low. Review itemized details.

formulas.label: Parts Markup Factor
formulas.description: Markup factor for OEM parts (30% premium) vs aftermarket.

formulas.label: Tax Amount
formulas.description: Sales tax on parts and supplies (labor is often tax-exempt).

formulas.label: Consistency Score
formulas.description: Score between 0 and 1 comparing quote to industry benchmark. Benchmark derived from shop type and repair category.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Labor Rate Premium
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Difference between quoted labor rate and benchmark rate for shop type.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Parts Markup Excess
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Extra cost due to OEM vs aftermarket parts beyond typical markup.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Shop Supplies Inflation
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Shop supplies fee above industry average (e.g., >3% of subtotal).

suggestedActions.label: Compare Labor Rates
suggestedActions.description: Request quotes from other shops to verify labor rate competitiveness.

suggestedActions.label: Negotiate Parts Cost
suggestedActions.description: Ask for aftermarket alternatives or price match on OEM parts.

suggestedActions.label: Review Shop Supplies
suggestedActions.description: Request itemization of shop supplies fee; typical range is 2-5% of subtotal.

suggestedActions.label: Get Second Opinion
suggestedActions.description: If consistency score is below 0.6, obtain a second quote.

dataConfidenceAdjusted.label: Data Confidence Adjusted Total
dataConfidenceAdjusted.description: Total quote adjusted by a confidence factor based on input completeness and consistency score.
